title: Implement Propose+Queue stage (stage 7/7)
description:
Implement the Propose+Queue stage of the ingestion pipeline (§6.1.1 stage 7).

**Inputs:** ExtractionBundle + MatchResults + vault snapshot
**Outputs:** Review Queue Items (SCH-007 compliant)
**Side effects:** Writes queue items under Inbox/ReviewQueue/.
**Errors:** ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: For each NEW claim, generates a Review Queue Item with item_type "claim_note" and proposed_action "promote_to_canon".
- AC-2: For CONTRADICTING matches, generates review items that remain human-review only (never auto-approved).
- AC-3: For claims in similarity band [0.70..0.85), generates queue items with merge/create recommendation requiring reviewer decision.
- AC-4: For EXACT matches, generates auto-approvable provenance-attachment items (if applicable).
- AC-5: Queue items include checks with at least provenance_present for claim-related items.
- AC-6: Queue items are written to Inbox/ReviewQueue/.
- AC-7: Queue items conform to SCH-007 schema.

## Spec References
- §6.1.1 (Stage 7: Propose+Queue)
- §4.2.7 SCH-007 (Review Queue Item schema)
- §8.1 REV-001 (Review queue generation — boundary; actual review logic is §8)

- depends_on_section: §8 (Review Queue and Promotion — downstream consumer)

title: Implement deterministic advisory confidence rubric (CONF-001)
description:
Implement the MVP1 deterministic advisory confidence rubric for extracted claims (§7.5, CONF-001).

Formula: confidence = clamp(0, 1, 0.40*provenance_quality + 0.30*extract_consistency + 0.20*dedupe_support + 0.10*source_reliability)

Factor definitions (MVP1):
- provenance_quality: 1.0 if provenance meets structured minima (SCH-003), else 0.0
- extract_consistency: 1.0 if extraction passes SCH-008 validation, else 0.0
- dedupe_support: 1.0 (EXACT), 0.8 (NEAR_DUPLICATE), 0.6 (SUPPORTING), 0.4 (NEW), 0.2 (CONTRADICTING)
- source_reliability: 0.5 constant in MVP1

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Re-running confidence calculation for the same fixture yields identical values (AC-CONF-001-1).
- AC-2: Claims missing required provenance fields are not emitted as valid claims; if present in intermediate data, their confidence is <= 0.4 and they are routed to human review (AC-CONF-001-2).
- AC-3: All factor values are deterministic and in [0..1].
- AC-4: Confidence guides review ordering but is NOT a Promotion decision.
- AC-5: source_reliability is hardcoded to 0.5 in MVP1.
- AC-6: Unit tests verify correct confidence for each match_class with valid/invalid provenance.

## Spec References
- §7.5 CONF-001 (Confidence rubric)
- §4.2.3 SCH-003 (Provenance schema)
- §4.2.8 SCH-008 (ExtractionBundle schema)

title: Implement match class assignment with similarity thresholds (DED-002)
description:
Implement the comparator that assigns exactly one Match Class per extracted claim using defined similarity thresholds (§7.2, DED-002).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Each extracted claim receives exactly one match_class in {EXACT, NEAR_DUPLICATE, SUPPORTING, CONTRADICTING, NEW} (DED-002).
- AC-2: Total Match Records across all match_groups equals total_extracted_claims (AC-DED-002-1).
- AC-3: Each Match Record includes match_class and a numeric similarity in [0..1] (AC-DED-002-2).
- AC-4: Similarity thresholds enforced: EXACT >= 0.97, NEAR_DUPLICATE in [0.85, 0.97), NEW candidate < 0.70.
- AC-5: Similarity computation is deterministic — same inputs produce same similarity scores.

## Spec References
- §7.2 DED-002

## Implementation Notes
- Threshold band [0.70..0.85) handling is covered by bd-1q3 (DED-003 merge rules), not this bead
- This bead focuses on the comparator/classifier, not the downstream merge behavior
close_reason: Completed: comparator.py with MatchClass enum, classify_similarity(), compare_claim(), compare_claims(), CompareResult grouping, Dice bigram similarity. 37 tests cover all 5 ACs. 376/376 pass.

title: Implement triage scoring with deterministic formula, buckets, and hysteresis
description:
## Context
Spec §12.4 MVP3 Resolution TODO-Q-MVP3-1 (council Option C): Governed deterministic triage lifecycle with hysteresis.

## Work
- Implement triage_score formula: `clamp01(0.45*conflict_factor + 0.25*support_gap + 0.20*novelty + 0.10*staleness)`.
- Implement bucket classification: dense (>=0.67), mixed (0.34-0.67), watery (<0.34).
- Implement hysteresis rules:
  - dense -> mixed only after 2 consecutive evaluations with score < 0.62.
  - watery -> mixed on first evaluation with score >= 0.42.
- Deterministic outputs: same input yields same bucket across runs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Triage score computation matches the specified formula exactly.
- Bucket thresholds and hysteresis transitions match spec.
- Fixture test demonstrates hysteresis prevents oscillation.
- All computations are deterministic and reproducible.

## Cross-section dependencies
- depends_on_section: §7 (conflict_factor, support_gap, novelty, staleness derivations)

title: Implement vault directory layout with scope boundaries (VLT-001)
description:
## Requirement
The system MUST treat the default vault layout directories as the authoritative boundary between Draft Scope and Canonical Scope (VLT-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-VLT-001-1: Without Promotion, the system writes only under Draft Scope directories: Inbox/, Reports/, Logs/, Indexes/, and Quarantine/.
- AC-VLT-001-2: Promotion applies approved changes by creating or modifying Notes only within Canonical Scope and updates `status` to `canon`.

## Spec Reference
§4.1

## Implementation Notes
- Canonical scope: Sources/, Claims/, Concepts/, Questions/, Projects/, MOCs/
- Draft scope: Inbox/Sources/, Inbox/ReviewQueue/, Inbox/ReviewDigest/
- Derived (durable): Reports/Delta/, Logs/Audit/
- Derived (rebuildable): Indexes/
- Derived (durable): Quarantine/
- Scope classification must be queryable programmatically (is_canonical_scope, is_draft_scope helpers)
- Foundation bead: many other beads depend on this scope definition
close_reason: Implemented vault_layout.py with ScopeClassification enum, CANONICAL_DIRS/DRAFT_DIRS constants, classify_scope/is_canonical_scope/is_draft_scope helpers. 44/44 tests pass covering both ACs. Full suite 155/155 green.

title: Enforce idempotent ingestion identity invariant (INV-005)
description:
## Requirement
Ingestion MUST be idempotent with respect to (Normalized Locator, Source Fingerprint) (INV-005).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-INV-005-1: Re-ingesting the same Source (same locator+fingerprint) reuses the same `source_id` and creates no duplicate canonical Claim Notes.
- AC-INV-005-2: Re-ingesting a Source with the same locator but different fingerprint produces a new Run ID and records revision lineage in the Delta Report, without overwriting prior Source Notes.

## Spec Reference
§3 INV-005

## Implementation Notes
- Source identity key: (normalized_locator, fingerprint)
- Lookup by normalized_locator to detect re-ingestion vs revision
- Fingerprint comparison determines same-content vs content-change
- Revision lineage: Delta Report source_revision.prior_fingerprint field
- depends_on_section: §4 (SCH-002 Source Note schema, SCH-006 Delta Report schema)
- depends_on_section: §6 (ingestion pipeline identity stage)
- depends_on_section: §7 (dedupe engine)

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC-SCH-002-2: Validator rejects `fingerprint` not matching `sha256:<64-hex>`.
- AC-SCH-002-3: Validator rejects Source Notes missing any required Source Note key.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.2

## Implementation Notes
- source_kind enum: url | pdf | doi | arxiv | highlights | book | text_bundle
- fingerprint format: sha256:<hex> (64 hex chars)
- normalized_locator must be deterministic for the same source
- Depends on SCH-001 (shared frontmatter validation)

title: Implement regression test suite for dedupe and idempotency edge cases (TST-R-001)

**Spec ref:** §13.5 TST-R-001

**Requirements:**

- Semantically identical but differently formatted claims (should match as EXACT or NEAR_DUPLICATE)

- AC-TST-R-001-2: Regression cases remain stable across refactors (only updated when intended behavior changes, with changelog note).
- AC-TST-R-001-3: All 5 minimum regression cases listed above are present as individual test cases with explicit pass/fail assertions.

title: Implement merge rules for each match class (DED-003)

title: Implement frontier command scoring engine (CMD-FRN-001, CMD-FRN-002)
description:
## Requirement
frontier MUST surface conflicts, weak support, and open questions when present (CMD-FRN-001), and scoring MUST be deterministic using the weighted formula with hardened factor derivations (CMD-FRN-002, Option B per council decision).

## Spec Reference
§5.2.7 CMD-FRN-001, CMD-FRN-002

## Inputs
- Optional: project (string), tags (array[string]), limit (int)

## Output data
- conflicts (array), weak_support (array), open_questions (array)
- reading_targets: array[{target, score, rationale, citations, factors}] (ranked)
- explanations: object (ranking factors)

## Side Effects
None (read-only).

## Errors
ERR_NO_FRONTIER_DATA

## Scoring Formula (CMD-FRN-002)
score = 100 * (0.35*conflict_factor + 0.25*support_gap + 0.20*goal_relevance + 0.10*novelty + 0.10*staleness)
- Each factor in [0..1], score clamped to [0..100]
- Ties: higher conflict_factor, older last_reviewed_at, lexical target id

## Factor Derivations (Option B - hardened deterministic)
- ref_ts = command reference timestamp (fixed in Deterministic Test Mode)
- review_ts(T) = last_reviewed_at when present, otherwise updated
- conflict_factor(T) = clamp01(contradict_count(T) / max(1, contradict_count(T) + support_count(T)))
- support_gap(T) = 1.0 - clamp01(support_count(T) / 3.0)
- goal_relevance(T): if no project/tags: 0.5; else: clamp01(0.6*project_match + 0.4*tag_overlap)
  - project_match: 1.0 if match, 0.0 if mismatch, 0.5 if omitted/absent
  - tag_overlap: |T.tags ∩ input.tags| / max(1, |input.tags|); 0.5 if omitted
- novelty(T) = clamp01(p75(linked_delta_novelty_30d(T)))
- staleness(T) = clamp01(days_between(ref_ts, review_ts(T)) / 45.0)

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-CMD-FRN-001-1: Seeded vault with contradiction and question returns conflicts.length>=1 and open_questions.length>=1.
- AC-CMD-FRN-001-2: reading_targets sorted by explicit numeric rank/score field.
- AC-CMD-FRN-002-1: Deterministic fixture yields byte-identical reading_targets ordering and scores on repeated runs.
- AC-CMD-FRN-002-2: Each reading target includes factors with all five components, values in [0..1].
- AC-CMD-FRN-002-3: Equal-score tie-break follows defined deterministic order and fallback behavior.

## Implementation Notes
- Most complex command in §5: requires graph analysis + deterministic scoring
- p75 = nearest-rank 75th percentile over sorted values; empty → 0.0
- depends_on_section: §4 (note schemas, claims, delta reports), §7 (match classes for contradiction data)
close_reason: Completed: frontier scoring engine in commands/frontier.py with all 5 factor derivations (Option B), p75, clamp01, deterministic ranking + tie-breaking. 54 tests covering all 5 ACs.

description:
Implement the MVP1 deterministic advisory confidence rubric for extracted claims (§7.5, CONF-001).

Formula: confidence = clamp(0, 1, 0.40*provenance_quality + 0.30*extract_consistency + 0.20*dedupe_support + 0.10*source_reliability)

Factor definitions (MVP1):
- provenance_quality: 1.0 if provenance meets structured minima (SCH-003), else 0.0
- extract_consistency: 1.0 if extraction passes SCH-008 validation, else 0.0
- dedupe_support: 1.0 (EXACT), 0.8 (NEAR_DUPLICATE), 0.6 (SUPPORTING), 0.4 (NEW), 0.2 (CONTRADICTING)
- source_reliability: 0.5 constant in MVP1

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Re-running confidence calculation for the same fixture yields identical values (AC-CONF-001-1).
- AC-2: Claims missing required provenance fields are not emitted as valid claims; if present in intermediate data, their confidence is <= 0.4 and they are routed to human review (AC-CONF-001-2).
- AC-3: All factor values are deterministic and in [0..1].
- AC-4: Confidence guides review ordering but is NOT a Promotion decision.
- AC-5: source_reliability is hardcoded to 0.5 in MVP1.
- AC-6: Unit tests verify correct confidence for each match_class with valid/invalid provenance.

## Spec References
- §7.5 CONF-001, §4.2.3 SCH-003, §4.2.8 SCH-008
close_reason: Completed: deterministic confidence rubric (CONF-001) with 58 tests covering all 6 ACs

title: Log egress content with payload digest and source file paths (SEC-002)
description:
## Context
§9.2 of the spec. The system must log what content was sent externally.

## Requirements
- SEC-002: Egress audit events include either:
  (a) a locally stored outbound payload reference, or
  (b) a payload digest plus the list of source file paths included.
- Egress audit events include a reason field (command name and user request context).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SEC-002-1: Egress audit events include either locally stored payload reference
  or payload digest plus source file paths list.
- AC-SEC-002-2: Egress audit events include a reason field.
close_reason: Extended egress.py with SEC-002: compute_payload_digest helper, payload_ref/payload_digest/source_paths/reason params on egress(). 16 new tests (43 total) covering AC-SEC-002-1 (payload ref or digest+paths) and AC-SEC-002-2 (reason field).

title: Implement deterministic claim canonicalization (DED-001)
description:
Implement deterministic claim canonicalization that produces stable canonical forms and extracted_claim_key hashes (§7.1, DED-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Same input claim text produces byte-identical canonical output across runs (AC-DED-001-1).
- AC-2: Canonicalization collapses whitespace and normalizes Unicode so cosmetic formatting differences in a golden pair produce identical canonical outputs (AC-DED-001-2).
- AC-3: extracted_claim_key is computed as "h-" + first_12_hex(sha256(canonical_form_utf8_bytes)) (DED-001).
- AC-4: For a golden claim pair with identical canonical form, computed extracted_claim_key matches exactly (AC-DED-001-3).
- AC-5: Unit tests verify determinism with at least 3 golden pairs covering: whitespace variants, Unicode normalization, and case/punctuation variants.

## Spec References
- §7.1 DED-001
close_reason: Completed: canonicalize.py with canonicalize() + extracted_claim_key(). 19 tests cover all 5 ACs: determinism, 3 golden pairs (whitespace/unicode/case-punctuation), key formula, edge cases. 231/231 tests pass.

title: Implement Concept Note schema validation (SCH-004)
description:
## Requirement
A Concept Note MUST include `term: string` in addition to shared schema (SCH-004).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-004-1: Validator rejects Concept Notes missing `term`.
- AC-SCH-004-2: Promotion to canon fails for Concept Notes that have zero outbound Obsidian Wikilinks (at least one resolved wikilink is required).

## Spec Reference
§4.2.4

## Implementation Notes
- Lightweight schema — extends shared frontmatter with single required field
- Promotion guard: must have at least one wikilink resolving to another note
- Depends on SCH-001 (shared schema), LNK-001 (wikilink resolution)

title: [BUG] Sanitize phone regex false positives on numeric strings
description: HIGH: Phone regex in sanitize.py matches any 8+ digit sequence, producing false positives on reference IDs, account numbers, database IDs. Corrupts legitimate data during egress sanitization. Fix: Add word boundary anchors and require separator characters (dash, dot, space, parenthesis) within the number.
close_reason: Fixed: added word boundary assertions and required separators to phone regex, eliminating false positives on numeric IDs. Tests pass.

title: Implement Normalize stage (stage 2/7)
description:
Implement the Normalize stage of the ingestion pipeline (§6.1.1 stage 2).

**Inputs:** RawSourcePayload
**Outputs:** NormalizedSource { normalized_text, normalized_locator, source_kind, source_ref, extracted_metadata }
**Side effects:** None (pure transform).
**Errors:** ERR_NORMALIZATION_FAILED

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Given a RawSourcePayload with URL source, produces a NormalizedSource with a canonicalized normalized_locator string.
- AC-2: Normalize is a pure function — identical RawSourcePayload inputs produce byte-identical NormalizedSource outputs.
- AC-3: On malformed input, returns ERR_NORMALIZATION_FAILED with stage="normalize".
- AC-4: extracted_metadata includes source_kind and source_ref passed through from input.

## Spec References
- §6.1.1 (Stage 2: Normalize), §6.1.2 (Stage Names: "normalize")

## Cross-section Dependencies
- depends_on_section: §4 (Normalized Locator definition)
close_reason: Completed: stages/normalize.py with NormalizedSource, URL locator canonicalization (lowercase, sort params, strip fragments/defaults), text normalization (CRLF, blank lines), bytes fallback. 18 tests cover all 4 ACs. 1007/1007 pass.

title: Implement Propose+Queue stage (stage 7/7)
description:
Implement the Propose+Queue stage of the ingestion pipeline (§6.1.1 stage 7).

**Inputs:** ExtractionBundle + MatchResults + vault snapshot
**Outputs:** Review Queue Items (SCH-007 compliant)
**Side effects:** Writes queue items under Inbox/ReviewQueue/.
**Errors:** ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: For each NEW claim, generates a Review Queue Item with item_type "claim_note" and proposed_action "promote_to_canon".
- AC-2: For CONTRADICTING matches, generates review items that remain human-review only (never auto-approved).
- AC-3: For claims in similarity band [0.70..0.85), generates queue items with merge/create recommendation requiring reviewer decision.
- AC-4: For EXACT matches, generates auto-approvable provenance-attachment items (if applicable).
- AC-5: Queue items include checks with at least provenance_present for claim-related items.
- AC-6: Queue items are written to Inbox/ReviewQueue/.
- AC-7: Queue items conform to SCH-007 schema.

## Spec References
- §6.1.1 (Stage 7: Propose+Queue), §4.2.7 SCH-007, §8.1 REV-001

## Cross-section Dependencies
- depends_on_section: §4 (SCH-007 Review Queue Item schema)
- depends_on_section: §8 (Review Queue and Promotion — downstream consumer)
close_reason: Completed: Propose+Queue stage (7/7) implemented with 29 tests. All passing.

title: Implement Question Note schema validation (SCH-005)
description:
## Requirement
A Question Note MUST include `question_text: string` in addition to shared schema (SCH-005).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-005-1: Validator rejects Question Notes missing `question_text` or with empty `question_text` after trimming.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.5

## Implementation Notes
- Lightweight schema — extends shared frontmatter with single required field
- Trim-then-check validation (same pattern as claim_text in SCH-003)
- Depends on SCH-001 (shared schema)

title: Implement pipeline orchestrator with stage-scoped error handling (PIPE-001)
description:
Implement the pipeline orchestrator that executes stages in order with stage-scoped error handling (§6.1, PIPE-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Pipeline executes stages in order: capture → normalize → fingerprint → extract → compare → delta → propose_queue.
- AC-2: When a stage fails, command returns error with stage set to the failing Stage Name from the canonical set (AC-PIPE-001-1).
- AC-3: No downstream semantic stage runs using outputs from a failed stage (AC-PIPE-001-2). E.g., Compare does not run if Extract fails.
- AC-4: After a stage failure, failure-finalization steps (audit events, quarantine, Delta Report with failure status) MAY still run.
- AC-5: Audit events reference only canonical Stage Names: capture, normalize, fingerprint, extract, compare, delta, propose_queue (PIPE-003).
- AC-6: Each stage receives typed input from the prior stage's output (not raw/unvalidated data).

## Spec References
- §6.1 PIPE-001 (stage-scoped failures)
- §6.1.2 PIPE-003 (canonical Stage Names)
- §6.3 PIPE-002 (artifact staging and atomicity)

title: Implement Note ID naming rules and filename alignment (NAM-001)
description:
## Requirement
Note `id` MUST match one of three patterns and Markdown filename MUST equal `<id>.md` (NAM-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-NAM-001-1: Validator rejects Notes where filename and `id` differ.
- AC-NAM-001-2: Validator rejects `id` strings outside the allowed patterns.
- AC-NAM-001-3: Machine-generated notes default to hybrid `<slug>--h-<12hex>` pattern unless migration compatibility mode is enabled.

## Spec Reference
§4.3.1

## Implementation Notes
- Allowed id patterns:
  - slug-only: ^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$
  - hash-only: ^h-[0-9a-f]{12,64}$
  - hybrid machine form: ^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*--h-[0-9a-f]{12}$
- Resolution TODO-Q-NAM-1: Config key `naming.compat_mode` with values `strict` (default) or `legacy_slug_generation`
  - strict: machine-generated notes MUST use hybrid form
  - legacy_slug_generation: slug-only allowed if no collision, else fallback to hybrid
  - Mode does NOT relax NAM-001 validation or filename/id equality
- ID generation utility: slug_from_text() + hash suffix generator
- Depends on SCH-001 (id is a shared frontmatter field)
close_reason: Implemented naming.py with is_valid_note_id, validate_note_id, validate_filename_id_match, generate_hybrid_id, generate_hash_id, slug_from_text. 52 tests in test_naming.py covering AC-NAM-001-1/2/3.

title: Build schema migration framework with forward migration and rollback
description:
## Context
Spec §11.2 MIG-002: Any breaking schema or layout change MUST include a migration and rollback procedure.

## Work
- Create a migration framework that can apply schema/layout migrations to a vault.
- Each migration must be versioned and include both forward and rollback steps.
- Migration must validate that all notes still pass schema checks after apply.
- Rollback must restore pre-migration state byte-for-byte for canonical notes.
- Store migration version in vault metadata so migrations are idempotent and trackable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-MIG-002-1: A migration test applies the migration to a fixture vault and validates that all Notes still pass schema checks.
- AC-MIG-002-2: A rollback test restores the pre-migration fixture state byte-for-byte for Canonical Notes.

## Cross-section dependencies
- depends_on_section: §4 (note schemas that migrations operate on)
- depends_on_section: §3 (system invariants preserved across migrations)
close_reason: Implemented migration.py: MigrationRegistry, migrate_note_content, migrate_vault_notes, rollback_vault_from_backups. 21 tests covering AC-MIG-002-1 (migration + schema validation) + AC-MIG-002-2 (byte-for-byte rollback). Full suite 1074/1074 pass.

title: Implement Output Envelope contract (IF-001)
description:
## Requirement
Every command response MUST return the Output Envelope (IF-001). This is the universal response schema shared by all commands.

## Spec Reference
§5.1 IF-001

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-IF-001-1: All commands return envelope keys exactly as specified: ok, command, timestamp, data, errors, warnings, trace.
- AC-IF-001-2: If a command fails, ok=false and errors.length>=1.
- AC-IF-001-3: Error objects always include code, message, and retryable.
- AC-IF-001-4: timestamp parses as ISO-8601 UTC and command equals the invoked command name.

## Implementation Notes
- Define OutputEnvelope, ErrorObject, and WarningObject as shared data models
- ErrorObject fields: code (string), message (string), details (object, optional), stage (string|null, optional), retryable (boolean)
- WarningObject fields: code (string), message (string), details (object, optional)
- All command handlers must wrap returns through this envelope
- This is a foundational bead: all command beads depend on it
close_reason: Already implemented and tested. models.py has full IF-001 contract with 25 passing tests.

title: Implement Delta Report schema and persistence (SCH-006)
description:
## Requirement
Each Ingestion Job MUST persist exactly one Delta Report per Run ID in YAML under Reports/Delta/ (SCH-006).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-006-1: After any ingestion attempt that begins extraction and produces a schema-valid Extraction Bundle, a Delta Report exists for the Run ID even if later stages fail.
- AC-SCH-006-2: Delta Report YAML always includes required top-level keys and includes empty arrays explicitly (keys present even if empty).
- AC-SCH-006-3: Validator rejects Delta Reports with `novelty_score` outside [0..1].
- AC-SCH-006-4: Each Match Record includes required keys and `match_class` equals its containing group key.
- AC-SCH-006-5: `pipeline_status` is `completed` on success, `failed_after_extraction` when failure occurs after extraction begins, `failed_before_extraction` otherwise.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.6

## Implementation Notes
- Required top-level keys: run_id, source_id, created_at, source_revision, pipeline_status, counts, novelty_score, match_groups, conflicts, warnings, failures, new_links, follow_up_questions
- source_revision: normalized_locator, fingerprint, prior_fingerprint (nullable)
- counts: total_extracted_claims, exact_count, near_duplicate_count, supporting_count, contradicting_count, new_count
- match_groups: EXACT, NEAR_DUPLICATE, SUPPORTING, CONTRADICTING, NEW (each an array)
- Match Record: extracted_claim_key, match_class, similarity (0-1), existing_claim_id (nullable for NEW)
- Conflict Record: new_extracted_claim_key, existing_claim_id, evidence object
- This is a complex schema — consider a dedicated DeltaReport builder class
- depends_on_section: §6 (pipeline stages determine when to write)
- depends_on_section: §7 (dedupe engine populates match_groups)

title: Implement reading-first review digest workflow with packet semantics (REV-001A)
description:
## Context
§8.1.1 of the spec. Review must support a reading-first workflow that groups queue items
by Source into Review Packets (SCH-009). This is the nightly review workflow.

## Requirements
- REV-001A: Default review unit is a Source packet with optional claim-level drill-down.
- Packet actions: approve_all, approve_selected, hold, reject (SCH-009).
- hold keeps queue items pending_review and records hold_until metadata.
- CONTRADICTING proposals remain human-review only, never auto-approved.
- Hold policy stored in Config/review_policy.yaml under hold_ttl_days (default 14).
- Every hold decision writes explicit hold_until into packet decision data (SCH-009)
  and review decision results (SCH-010).
- Resurfacing is deterministic at review_digest time: held items re-included when
  hold_until <= digest_date.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-REV-001A-1: Generated digest includes packet summaries, claim cards (when enabled),
  citations, and canonical-impact descriptions for each Source packet.
- AC-REV-001A-2: Applying packet actions through review and graduate --from_digest yields
  deterministic queue and promotion outcomes for a deterministic fixture.
- AC-REV-001A-3: Hold decisions resurface after configured hold TTL (14 days).

## Cross-section Dependencies
- depends_on_section: §4.2.9 (SCH-009 Review Digest and Packet schema)
- depends_on_section: §5.2.4 (review_digest command contract)
close_reason: Completed: review workflow with packet semantics, hold/resurface, contradicting-skip logic. 35 tests covering all 3 ACs

title: Implement Review Digest and Packet schema (SCH-009)
description:
## Requirement
`review_digest` MUST persist Review Packets as YAML under Inbox/ReviewDigest/, one per Source (SCH-009).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-009-1: For a digest with N Sources, exactly N packet YAML files are created and each validates against this schema.
- AC-SCH-009-2: In deterministic test mode, packet ordering and contents are stable for the same Vault snapshot.
- AC-SCH-009-3: Validator rejects packet files with `action=approve_selected` that omit `approved_queue_ids` or include ids not in `queue_ids`.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.9

## Implementation Notes
- Required keys: packet_id, digest_date (YYYY-MM-DD), created_at, source_id, run_ids (non-empty), queue_ids (non-empty), decision (nullable)
- decision object (when non-null): action (approve_all|approve_selected|hold|reject), actor, decided_at, reason (nullable)
- approve_selected requires approved_queue_ids (non-empty)
- hold requires hold_until (YYYY-MM-DD)
- depends_on_section: §8 (review queue commands)

title: Implement shared frontmatter schema validation (SCH-001)
description:
## Requirement
Every Note MUST include the shared frontmatter keys: type, id, status, created, updated (SCH-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-001-1: A schema validator rejects any Note missing a required shared key and reports which key is missing.
- AC-SCH-001-2: `created` and `updated` parse as UTC ISO-8601 and validator rejects invalid formats.
- AC-SCH-001-3: If `confidence` exists, validator rejects values outside [0.0..1.0].
- AC-SCH-001-4: If `last_reviewed_at` exists, validator rejects invalid datetime formats.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.1

## Implementation Notes
- Required keys: type (enum), id (string), status (enum), created (datetime), updated (datetime)
- Optional keys: tags (list[string]), confidence (number 0-1), last_reviewed_at (datetime)
- Validators MUST ignore unknown keys (forward compatibility)
- type enum: source | claim | concept | question | project | moc
- status enum: draft | reviewed | canon
- This is the foundation schema validator — all note-type-specific validators build on it

title: Implement graduate command contract (CMD-GRD-001)
description:
## Requirement
graduate MUST apply Promotions only for approved queue items and MUST be atomic per queue item (CMD-GRD-001). This is the sole path for Draft->Canon promotion per INV-002.

## Spec Reference
§5.2.5 CMD-GRD-001

## Inputs
- One of: queue_id (string), queue_item_paths (array[string]), all_approved (boolean), from_digest (string, apply packet decisions from digest path)
- Optional: dry_run (boolean), strict (boolean)

## Output data
- promoted: array[{queue_id, from_path, to_path}]
- rejected: array[{queue_id, reason}]
- skipped: array[{queue_id, reason}]
- audit_event_ids: array[string]

## Side Effects
- Applies approved Promotions, moving artifacts into Canonical Scope, updating statuses
- Appends audit events

## Errors
ERR_QUEUE_ITEM_INVALID, ERR_QUEUE_IMMUTABLE, ERR_PROVENANCE_MISSING, ERR_PROMOTION_CONFLICT, ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-CMD-GRD-001-1: If a queue item fails validation, that item has no canonical changes while other valid items may still be promoted (per-item atomicity).
- AC-CMD-GRD-001-2: On success, each promoted Note has status: canon and resides in a Canonical Directory.
- AC-CMD-GRD-001-3: Any graduate with dry_run=false rejects strict=false with ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION (non-Strict only permitted when dry_run=true).
- AC-CMD-GRD-001-4: from_digest applies only items explicitly approved by packet decisions; held items remain pending.

## Implementation Notes
- Per-item atomicity: one failure must not block others
- strict=false only allowed with dry_run=true (safety constraint)
- Must integrate with audit log (§9) and Git Mode (§8.3.1) when enabled
- depends_on_section: §3 (INV-002 human authority), §4 (schemas), §8 (review queue + Git Mode), §9 (audit)
close_reason: Implemented graduate.py with GraduateInput, per-item promotion, type-based canonical path routing. Enforces strict constraint (AC-3), per-item atomicity (AC-1), status:canon on promotion (AC-2), held items skipped (AC-4). 14/14 tests pass. Full suite 973/973 green.

title: Implement Capture stage (stage 1/7)
description:
Implement the Capture stage of the ingestion pipeline (§6.1.1 stage 1).

**Inputs:** Source input (url / pdf_path / id / text_bundle)
**Outputs:** RawSourcePayload { bytes|text, media_type, source_ref, source_kind }
**Side effects:** May create transient capture cache entries in Draft Scope only.
**Errors:** ERR_CAPTURE_FAILED, ERR_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Given a valid URL source input, produces a RawSourcePayload with non-empty bytes/text, correct media_type, and source_kind="url".
- AC-2: Given a valid PDF path, produces a RawSourcePayload with source_kind="pdf".
- AC-3: Given an unsupported source type, returns structured error with code ERR_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE.
- AC-4: On network/IO failure, returns ERR_CAPTURE_FAILED with stage="capture" in the error.
- AC-5: Any cache entries written are in Draft Scope only (not Canonical Scope).

## Spec References
- §6.1.1 (Stage 1: Capture)
- §6.1.2 (Stage Names: "capture")
- PIPE-001 (stage-scoped failures)

## Cross-section Dependencies
- depends_on_section: §4 (Source Kind definitions, Vault layout for Draft Scope)
- depends_on_section: §5 (CLI ingest command interface)
close_reason: Completed: stages/capture.py with SourceKind enum, SourceInput, RawSourcePayload, capture() dispatching to URL/PDF/text_bundle handlers. Structured errors (ERR_CAPTURE_FAILED, ERR_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE) with stage='capture'. 17 tests cover all 5 ACs. 870/870 pass.

title: Implement integration tests for pipeline chain and draft/canon boundaries (TST-I-001)
description:
Implement integration test suite covering pipeline stage chain and Draft/Canon boundary enforcement.

**Spec ref:** §13.2 TST-I-001

**Requirements:**
Integration tests MUST cover the pipeline stage chain and enforce Draft/Canon boundaries.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-TST-I-001-1: A full pipeline run from capture→delta→queue completes for each supported source kind fixture
- AC-TST-I-001-2: A boundary test verifies no writes occur under Canonical Scope without Promotion (AC-INV-002-1)
- AC-TST-I-001-3: review transition tests enforce legal transitions and immutable-state failures
- AC-TST-I-001-4: review_digest tests verify Source packet grouping and packet schema (SCH-009)

**depends_on_section:** §3 (system invariants for INV-002), §5 (CLI interfaces), §6 (ingestion pipeline), §8 (review queue/promotion)
close_reason: Implemented test_integration_pipeline.py: 26 tests covering AC-TST-I-001-1 (full pipeline capture→delta→queue), AC-TST-I-001-2 (draft/canon boundary — no canonical writes without promotion), AC-TST-I-001-3 (review state transitions and immutability), AC-TST-I-001-4 (review digest packet grouping and SCH-009 schema). 26/26 tests pass.

title: [BUG] Audit JSONL non-atomic writes corrupt under concurrent access
description: HIGH: audit.py emit_event uses Python buffered append mode. Events with large details dicts can exceed PIPE_BUF (4096 bytes), causing interleaved writes that produce corrupt JSONL when multiple processes call emit_event concurrently. Fix: Use os.open with O_WRONLY|O_APPEND|O_CREAT for POSIX atomic appends, or use file locking for large events.
close_reason: done

title: Implement source identity index for idempotency (IDM-001)
description:
Implement the source index mapping (normalized_locator, fingerprint) -> source_id for idempotent ingestion (§6.4, IDM-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: System maintains a source index mapping (normalized_locator, fingerprint) -> source_id.
- AC-2: Ingesting an identical fixture twice returns the same source_id and sets data.idempotency.reused_source_id=true on the second run (AC-IDM-001-1).
- AC-3: Ingesting a changed-content fixture with the same normalized_locator returns a different fingerprint and records prior_fingerprint in the Delta Report source_revision (AC-IDM-001-2).
- AC-4: Source index is persisted under Indexes/ and survives process restart.
- AC-5: Index lookups are O(1) or O(log n) — not full vault scans.

## Spec References
- §6.4 IDM-001, INV-005

## Cross-section Dependencies
- depends_on_section: §3 (INV-005 idempotency invariant)
- depends_on_section: §4 (Indexes/ layout)
close_reason: Implemented source_index.py with SourceIndex class: lookup(), register(), get_source_id(), get_prior_fingerprint(), size property. Persistent JSON at Indexes/source_identity_index.json. O(1) locator lookup. 16/16 tests pass in test_source_index.py.

title: Implement deterministic claim canonicalization (DED-001)
description:
Implement deterministic claim canonicalization that produces stable canonical forms and extracted_claim_key hashes (§7.1, DED-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Same input claim text produces byte-identical canonical output across runs (AC-DED-001-1).
- AC-2: Canonicalization collapses whitespace and normalizes Unicode so cosmetic formatting differences in a golden pair produce identical canonical outputs (AC-DED-001-2).
- AC-3: extracted_claim_key is computed as "h-" + first_12_hex(sha256(canonical_form_utf8_bytes)) (DED-001).
- AC-4: For a golden claim pair with identical canonical form, computed extracted_claim_key matches exactly (AC-DED-001-3).
- AC-5: Unit tests verify determinism with at least 3 golden pairs covering: whitespace variants, Unicode normalization, and case/punctuation variants.

## Spec References
- §7.1 DED-001 (Claim canonicalization)

title: Implement promotion semantics with strict validation and audit (REV-003)
description:
## Context
§8.3 of the spec. Promotion via graduate is the critical gate that moves approved
drafts into canonical scope. This is the core human-authority enforcement mechanism.

## Requirements
- REV-003: graduate must:
  1) Validate schemas (SCH-001..SCH-010) in Strict Mode for promoted items.
  2) Update promoted Notes to status: canon.
  3) Write promoted Notes into Canonical Scope.
  4) Append audit entries for all affected files (§9.1).
- Per-item atomicity: if one item fails validation, other valid items may still promote.
- graduate with dry_run=false rejects strict=false with ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION.
- from_digest applies only items explicitly approved by packet decisions; held items
  remain pending.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-REV-003-1: After successful Promotion, every promoted Note validates and has status: canon.
- AC-REV-003-2: Audit log contains entry listing promoted paths and actor identifier.
- AC-REV-003-3: graduate attempt that would mutate Canonical Scope while strict=false
  fails with ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION.

## Cross-section Dependencies
- depends_on_section: §3 INV-002 (human authority over canon)
- depends_on_section: §9.1 (audit logging for promotions)
- depends_on_section: §5.2.5 (graduate command contract)
close_reason: Completed: Added audit logging to graduate(), actor field to GraduateInput. 17 tests in test_graduate_promotion.py cover all 3 ACs. 1693/1695 pass (2 pre-existing failures).

title: Implement match class assignment with similarity thresholds (DED-002)
description:
Implement the comparator that assigns exactly one Match Class per extracted claim using defined similarity thresholds (§7.2, DED-002).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Each extracted claim receives exactly one match_class in {EXACT, NEAR_DUPLICATE, SUPPORTING, CONTRADICTING, NEW} (DED-002).
- AC-2: Total Match Records across all match_groups equals total_extracted_claims (AC-DED-002-1).
- AC-3: Each Match Record includes match_class and a numeric similarity in [0..1] (AC-DED-002-2).
- AC-4: Similarity thresholds are enforced: EXACT >= 0.97, NEAR_DUPLICATE in [0.85, 0.97), NEW candidate < 0.70.
- AC-5: Similarity in [0.70..0.85) is assigned a match_class (implementation determines which) — downstream merge rules (DED-003) handle the reviewer-decision routing for this band.
- AC-6: Similarity computation is deterministic — same inputs produce same similarity scores.

## Spec References
- §7.2 DED-002 (Match class assignment)

## Note
The [0.70..0.85) ambiguity band routing to reviewer decisions is handled by DED-003 (merge rules), not this bead. This bead assigns match_class only; it does not determine reviewer routing.

title: Implement Extract stage (stage 4/7)
description:
Implement the Extract stage of the ingestion pipeline (§6.1.1 stage 4).

**Inputs:** NormalizedSource
**Outputs:** ExtractionBundle artifact (SCH-008 compliant)

**Errors:** ERR_EXTRACTION_FAILED, ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION

- AC-4: ExtractionBundle is written to Inbox/Sources/ directory.
- AC-5: Claims include claim_text, provenance with source_id and locator.
- AC-6: On extraction failure, returns ERR_EXTRACTION_FAILED with stage="extract".
- AC-7: Schema-invalid extraction output returns ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION with stage="extract".

title: Implement spec lint: interface completeness check (LINT-003)
description:
Implement a spec-lint pass that validates every required interface specifies inputs, outputs, side effects, and errors.

**Spec ref:** §14 LINT-003

**Requirements:**
Every interface described as required MUST specify inputs, outputs, side effects, and errors.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-LINT-003-1: A spec-lint pass flags any required command or Stage interface missing any of the four components (inputs, outputs, side effects, errors)

**Implementation notes:**
- Identify required command definitions and Stage interface blocks in the spec
- For each, verify presence of all four components: inputs, outputs, side effects, errors
- Report missing components with section refs and line numbers

title: Implement review_digest command contract (CMD-RDG-001)
description:
## Requirement
review_digest MUST produce a reading-first artifact grouped by Source, with per-Source Review Packets that support deterministic downstream decision application (CMD-RDG-001).

## Spec Reference
§5.2.4 CMD-RDG-001

## Inputs
- Optional: date (string, YYYY-MM-DD, default=today), run_ids (array[string]), limit_sources (int), include_claim_cards (boolean, default=true)

## Output data
- digest_path (string), packet_paths (array[string]), source_count (int), pending_item_count (int)

## Side Effects
- Writes digest under Inbox/ReviewDigest/
- Writes per-Source packet records (SCH-009)

## Errors
ERR_REVIEW_DIGEST_EMPTY, ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-CMD-RDG-001-1: Digest with pending items includes one Review Packet per Source; each packet includes queue_ids and run_ids (SCH-009).
- AC-CMD-RDG-001-2: Packet decisions support exactly approve_all, approve_selected, hold, and reject (SCH-009).
- AC-CMD-RDG-001-3: Digest generation is deterministic for the same snapshot in Deterministic Test Mode (§13.4).

## Resolution (TODO-Q-RDG-1)
- Authoritative invocation surface is the review_digest command/API contract itself
- May be invoked manually, by external scheduler, or via client integration — all paths MUST use same semantics and artifact schemas
- Built-in scheduler not required by spec
- Invocations MUST record actor provenance (user:<id>, scheduler:<id>, client:<id>)

## Implementation Notes
- Digest is the primary reading interface for human review
- Deterministic output required in test mode
- depends_on_section: §4 (SCH-009 packet schema), §8 (review queue), §13 (deterministic mode)
close_reason: Completed: review_digest contract in commands/review_digest.py with PacketAction, ReviewPacket, ReviewDigestInput, validate/execute. 19 tests covering all 3 ACs.

title: Implement delta command contract (CMD-DEL-001)
description:
## Requirement
delta command MUST return match group arrays for all Match Classes and include counts (CMD-DEL-001). This is a read-only command that surfaces delta report data.

## Spec Reference
§5.2.2 CMD-DEL-001

## Inputs
- One of: source_id (string), delta_report_path (string)
- Optional: strict (boolean)

## Output data
- run_id, source_id, counts (object), match_groups (object), conflicts (array), new_links (array), follow_up_questions (array), citations (array)

## Side Effects
None (read-only).

## Errors
ERR_SOURCE_NOT_FOUND, ERR_DELTA_NOT_FOUND, ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-CMD-DEL-001-1: data.match_groups includes keys EXACT, NEAR_DUPLICATE, SUPPORTING, CONTRADICTING, NEW.
- AC-CMD-DEL-001-2: data.counts.total_extracted_claims equals the sum of the five match class counts in the Delta Report.

## Implementation Notes
- Read-only: loads and formats existing Delta Report data
- Strict mode applies (read-only downgrade semantics per IF-003)
- depends_on_section: §4 (SCH-006 Delta Report schema), §7 (Match Classes)
close_reason: Completed: delta command contract in commands/delta.py with MatchClass, DeltaInput, make_counts, make_empty_match_groups. 16 tests covering both ACs.

title: Enforce provenance required for imported claims invariant (INV-004)
description:
## Requirement
Imported Claims MUST include Provenance sufficient to trace back to a Source and Locator (INV-004).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-INV-004-1: Schema validation fails for any Claim Note missing required Provenance fields (per SCH-003).
- AC-INV-004-2: Promotion refuses any Claim Note missing required Provenance, returning `ERR_PROVENANCE_MISSING`, and does not mutate Canonical Scope.

## Spec Reference
§3 INV-004

## Implementation Notes
- Provenance requires: source_id, source_ref, locator (with kind-specific sub-fields)
- Validation must run both at ingestion time and at promotion time
- Error code `ERR_PROVENANCE_MISSING` must be defined in error catalog
- depends_on_section: §4 (SCH-003 defines Claim Note schema with provenance)

title: Store audit logs as newline-delimited JSON (JSONL) under Logs/Audit/ (AUD-002)
description:
## Context
§9.1 of the spec. Defines the storage format for audit logs.

## Requirements
- AUD-002: Audit logs stored as newline-delimited JSON (JSONL) under Logs/Audit/.
- One audit event per line.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC-AUD-002-2: Appending new events does not change byte content of previous lines.

title: Implement Review Queue Item schema (SCH-007)
description:
## Requirement
Each Review Queue Item MUST be persisted as YAML under Inbox/ReviewQueue/ with required keys (SCH-007).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-007-1: System refuses to mutate a non-`pending_review` queue item except via explicit state transition operations, returning `ERR_QUEUE_IMMUTABLE`.
- AC-SCH-007-2: `graduate` in Dry Run mode lists validation results for each queue item without changing any files.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.7

## Implementation Notes
- Required keys: queue_id, run_id, item_type, target_path, proposed_action, status, created_at, checks
- item_type enum: source_note | claim_note | concept_note | question_note | link_proposal | merge_proposal
- proposed_action enum: create | promote_to_canon | merge | link | reject
- status enum: pending_review | approved | rejected
- Immutability guard: only pending_review items can be modified, except through explicit review operations
- Error code `ERR_QUEUE_IMMUTABLE` must be defined
- depends_on_section: §8 (review queue and promotion)

title: Implement Note ID naming rules and filename alignment (NAM-001)
description:
## Requirement
Note `id` MUST match one of three patterns and Markdown filename MUST equal `<id>.md` (NAM-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-NAM-001-1: Validator rejects Notes where filename and `id` differ.
- AC-NAM-001-2: Validator rejects `id` strings outside the allowed patterns.
- AC-NAM-001-3: Machine-generated notes default to hybrid `<slug>--h-<12hex>` pattern unless migration compatibility mode is enabled.

## Spec Reference
§4.3.1

## Implementation Notes
- Allowed id patterns:
  - slug-only: ^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$
  - hash-only: ^h-[0-9a-f]{12,64}$
  - hybrid machine form: ^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*--h-[0-9a-f]{12}$
- Resolution TODO-Q-NAM-1: Config key `naming.compat_mode` with values `strict` (default) or `legacy_slug_generation`
  - strict: machine-generated notes MUST use hybrid form
  - legacy_slug_generation: slug-only allowed if no collision, else fallback to hybrid
  - Mode does NOT relax NAM-001 validation or filename/id equality
- ID generation utility: slug_from_text() + hash suffix generator
- Depends on SCH-001 (id is a shared frontmatter field)

title: Implement quarantine with diagnostic sidecar for invalid artifacts
description:
## Context
Spec §10.2 ERR-002: Invalid or partial artifacts MUST be placed in `Quarantine/` with a diagnostic sidecar.

## Work
- Create quarantine logic that moves corrupted/invalid artifacts to `Quarantine/` directory.
- Generate a diagnostic sidecar file alongside each quarantined artifact containing: the parse error, the affected original path, and a timestamp.
- Ensure the original corrupted canonical file (if any) is NOT overwritten — quarantine is a copy, not a move of canonical content.
- Wire quarantine into each pipeline stage's error handling path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-ERR-002-1: A corrupted frontmatter fixture results in a quarantined copy and a diagnostic file containing the parse error and the affected original path.
- AC-ERR-002-2: The original corrupted canonical file (if any) is not overwritten.

## Cross-section dependencies
- depends_on_section: §4 (vault layout, Quarantine/ directory in Draft Scope)
- depends_on_section: §6 (pipeline stages where quarantine triggers)

title: Validate MVP2 end-to-end: review lifecycle, graduate, frontier scoring
description:
## Context
Spec §12.2 MVP2-001: MVP2 capability bundle validation. MVP2 MUST include review queue lifecycle enforcement with graduate, promotion updates to Canonical Scope with audit records, frontier output for seeded contradictions/questions using deterministic scoring formula, and stable context/frontier retrieval over canonical wikilink graph.

## Work
- Create integration test enforcing invalid queue transitions fail with ERR_QUEUE_IMMUTABLE.
- Create frontier fixture test: seeded fixture yields non-empty `conflicts` and `open_questions`.
- Verify deterministic frontier scoring: fixture yields same scores and ordering across repeated runs.
- Verify promotion writes audit records and updates Canonical Scope correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-MVP2-001-1: Integration test enforces invalid queue transitions fail with ERR_QUEUE_IMMUTABLE.
- AC-MVP2-001-2: Frontier seeded fixture yields non-empty `conflicts` and `open_questions`.
- AC-MVP2-001-3: Frontier fixture yields deterministic scores and ordering across repeated runs.

## Cross-section dependencies
- depends_on_section: §8 (review queue lifecycle, graduate command)
- depends_on_section: §9 (audit records on promotion)
- depends_on_section: §5 (frontier CLI command)
- depends_on_section: §7 (frontier scoring formula)

title: source_index.py _load() missing error handling for corrupted JSON
description: SourceIndex._load() in src/mycelium/source_index.py reads JSON with json.loads() but has no error handling for corrupted/malformed JSON files. If the index file gets corrupted (e.g., partial write from a crash), _load() will raise an unhandled json.JSONDecodeError. Per INV-001 invariant, it should gracefully degrade - either return an empty index (with a warning logged) or raise a structured error. Found during adversarial peer review.

title: Enforce human authority over canon invariant (INV-002)
description:
## Requirement
Canonical Notes MUST NOT be created or modified without an explicit Promotion action via `graduate` (INV-002).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-INV-002-1: Running ingestion and other commands without Promotion produces no diffs under Canonical Scope and does not change any Note with `status: canon`.
- AC-INV-002-2: Attempted writes targeting Canonical Scope without Promotion return structured error `ERR_CANON_WRITE_FORBIDDEN` and produce no file mutation.

## Spec Reference
§3 INV-002

## Implementation Notes
- Canonical scope directories: Sources/, Claims/, Concepts/, Questions/, Projects/, MOCs/
- Write guard must intercept all filesystem writes and check target path against canonical scope
- Error code `ERR_CANON_WRITE_FORBIDDEN` must be defined in error catalog
- depends_on_section: §4 (VLT-001 defines canonical vs draft scope boundaries)
- depends_on_section: §8 (graduate command implements Promotion)
close_reason: Added validate_canon_protection to invariants.py. Guards canonical scope writes and canon-status note modifications with ERR_CANON_WRITE_FORBIDDEN. Promotion bypass via is_promotion flag. 14 new tests (41 total in test_invariants.py). Full suite 417/417 green.

title: Implement Review Decision Record schema (SCH-010)

title: [SEC] MCP server: TOCTOU race on progress.yaml read-modify-write
description: HIGH: _update_progress reads YAML, modifies in memory, writes back with no file locking. In multi-agent system, two concurrent update_progress calls silently clobber each other. Fix: Use advisory file locking (fcntl.flock) or atomic write-then-rename pattern.
close_reason: Fixed: added fcntl.flock() advisory file locking around progress.yaml read-modify-write in _update_progress. Tests pass.

title: Implement Normalize stage (stage 2/7)
description:
Implement the Normalize stage of the ingestion pipeline (§6.1.1 stage 2).

**Inputs:** RawSourcePayload
**Outputs:** NormalizedSource { normalized_text, normalized_locator, source_kind, source_ref, extracted_metadata }
**Side effects:** None (pure transform).
**Errors:** ERR_NORMALIZATION_FAILED

- AC-2: Normalize is a pure function — identical RawSourcePayload inputs produce byte-identical NormalizedSource outputs.

title: Enforce idempotent ingestion identity invariant (INV-005)
description:
## Requirement
Ingestion MUST be idempotent with respect to (Normalized Locator, Source Fingerprint) (INV-005).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-INV-005-1: Re-ingesting the same Source (same locator+fingerprint) reuses the same `source_id` and creates no duplicate canonical Claim Notes.
- AC-INV-005-2: Re-ingesting a Source with the same locator but different fingerprint produces a new Run ID and records revision lineage in the Delta Report, without overwriting prior Source Notes.

## Spec Reference
§3 INV-005

## Implementation Notes
- Source identity key: (normalized_locator, fingerprint)
- Lookup by normalized_locator to detect re-ingestion vs revision
- Fingerprint comparison determines same-content vs content-change
- Revision lineage: Delta Report source_revision.prior_fingerprint field
- depends_on_section: §4 (SCH-002 Source Note schema, SCH-006 Delta Report schema)
- depends_on_section: §6 (ingestion pipeline identity stage)
- depends_on_section: §7 (dedupe engine)

title: Implement Review Digest and Packet schema (SCH-009)
description:
## Requirement
`review_digest` MUST persist Review Packets as YAML under Inbox/ReviewDigest/, one per Source (SCH-009).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-009-1: For a digest with N Sources, exactly N packet YAML files are created and each validates against this schema.
- AC-SCH-009-2: In deterministic test mode, packet ordering and contents are stable for the same Vault snapshot.
- AC-SCH-009-3: Validator rejects packet files with `action=approve_selected` that omit `approved_queue_ids` or include ids not in `queue_ids`.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.9

## Implementation Notes
- Required keys: packet_id, digest_date (YYYY-MM-DD), created_at, source_id, run_ids (non-empty), queue_ids (non-empty), decision (nullable)
- decision object (when non-null): action (approve_all|approve_selected|hold|reject), actor, decided_at, reason (nullable)
- approve_selected requires approved_queue_ids (non-empty)
- hold requires hold_until (YYYY-MM-DD)
- depends_on_section: §8 (review queue commands)
close_reason: Implemented review_packet.py with validate_review_packet(), validate_review_packet_strict(), build_review_packet(), save_review_packet(), load_review_packet(), list_review_packets(). Covers AC-SCH-009-1 (N sources = N packets), AC-SCH-009-2 (deterministic output), AC-SCH-009-3 (approve_selected constraints). 64/64 tests pass.

title: Implement skip-list lifecycle with entry criteria, safety cap, and auto-resurface
description:
## Context
Spec §12.4 MVP3 Resolution TODO-Q-MVP3-1: Skip-list governed lifecycle (council B/C hybrid).

## Work
- Implement skip-list entry requiring ALL of:
  - 3 consecutive watery evaluations
  - conflict_factor == 0
  - zero open-question references
  - target not manually pinned
  - target age >= 14 days
- Store skip-list metadata per target: skip_since, skip_reason, next_review_at (default skip_since + 30 days).
- Implement skip-list removal on first of: any new conflict reference, any new open-question reference, manual unskip, next_review_at reached (auto-resurface and re-evaluate).
- Enforce safety cap: skip-list may include at most 20% of active targets in a snapshot.
- Skipped targets remain retrievable with include_skip=true.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Skip-list entry enforces all 5 conditions simultaneously.
- Skip-list removal triggers on each of the 4 specified events.
- Safety cap at 20% is enforced; attempting to exceed it fails gracefully.
- Skipped targets are retrievable with include_skip=true flag.

## Cross-section dependencies
- depends_on_section: §7 (conflict_factor, open-question reference data)

title: Implement egress policy with allowlist and blocklist checks (SEC-001)
description:
## Context
§9.2 of the spec. External egress must be governed by an Egress Policy with
allowlist/blocklist checks and optional sanitization.

## Requirements
- SEC-001: Egress governed by allowlist and blocklist (glob patterns, vault-relative).
- Default allowlist: Sources/**, Claims/**, Concepts/**, Questions/**, Projects/**,
  MOCs/**, Inbox/ReviewDigest/**, Reports/Delta/**.
- Default blocklist: Logs/Audit/**, Indexes/**, Quarantine/**, **/.git/**,
  **/*.key, **/*.pem, **/*secret*.
- Blocklisted path fails with ERR_EGRESS_POLICY_BLOCK and emits egress_blocked audit event.
- Allowlisted payload emits egress_completed audit event with bytes_sent and destination.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SEC-001-1: Egressing a blocklisted path fails with ERR_EGRESS_POLICY_BLOCK and
  emits egress_blocked audit event.
- AC-SEC-001-2: Egressing an allowlisted test payload emits egress_completed audit event
  including bytes_sent and destination identifier.

## Cross-section Dependencies
- depends_on_section: §9.1 (audit logging for egress events)
close_reason: Implemented egress.py with check_egress_policy, egress function, EgressPolicyError. Default allowlist/blocklist per §9.2. 27 tests in test_egress.py covering AC-SEC-001-1/2.

title: Define future command stubs for connect, trace, ideas (CMD-FUT-001)

title: Generate review queue items for all canonical-impacting proposals (REV-001)
description:

title: Implement performance bench tests enforcing PERF-001 thresholds (TST-P-001)
description:
Implement bench test suite enforcing PERF-001 thresholds with reproducible p95 reports.

**Spec ref:** §13.6 TST-P-001, §12.3 PERF-001

**Requirements:**
Bench tests MUST enforce PERF-001 thresholds and produce reproducible p95 reports.

PERF-001 specific p95 targets:
- ingest(url_basic) p95 <= 60s
- ingest(pdf_basic) p95 <= 120s
- delta by delta_report_path p95 <= 5s
- frontier on seeded medium fixture p95 <= 8s

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-TST-P-001-1: Bench suite runs in CI (or scheduled local gate) and records p50/p95/p99 plus pass/fail per target.
- AC-TST-P-001-2: Any p95 threshold breach blocks release unless explicitly waived with a changelog rationale.
- AC-TST-P-001-3: Benchmark results include fixture id, run count, and hardware profile metadata (AC-PERF-001-2).

**depends_on_section:** §6 (pipeline perf targets), §7 (delta engine perf), §12 (milestones define PERF-001 thresholds)

title: Enforce explicit review state transitions via review command (REV-002)
description:

title: Implement pipeline orchestrator with stage-scoped error handling (PIPE-001)
description:
Implement the pipeline orchestrator that executes stages in order with stage-scoped error handling (§6.1, PIPE-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Pipeline executes stages in order: capture → normalize → fingerprint → extract → compare → delta → propose_queue.
- AC-2: When a stage fails, command returns error with stage set to the failing Stage Name from the canonical set (AC-PIPE-001-1).
- AC-3: No downstream semantic stage runs using outputs from a failed stage (AC-PIPE-001-2).
- AC-4: After a stage failure, failure-finalization steps (audit events, quarantine, Delta Report with failure status) MAY still run.
- AC-5: Audit events reference only canonical Stage Names: capture, normalize, fingerprint, extract, compare, delta, propose_queue (PIPE-003).
- AC-6: Each stage receives typed input from the prior stage's output (not raw/unvalidated data).

## Spec References
- §6.1 PIPE-001, §6.1.2 PIPE-003, §6.3 PIPE-002

title: Implement Source Note schema validation (SCH-002)

§4.2.2

Full suite 373/376 pass (3 pre-existing failures in test_comparator.py).

title: Implement deterministic novelty scoring (DEL-002)
description:
Implement deterministic novelty_score computation using only Delta Report counts (§7.4, DEL-002).

Formula: novelty_score = (new_count + contradicting_count) / max(1, total_extracted_claims)

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: For any Delta Report, recomputing the formula from counts matches stored novelty_score exactly within floating-point tolerance of 1e-9 (AC-DEL-002-1).
- AC-2: novelty_score equals 0 when total_extracted_claims == 0 (AC-DEL-002-2).
- AC-3: novelty_score is always in [0..1] range (SCH-006 validation: AC-SCH-006-3).
- AC-4: Computation uses only new_count + contradicting_count from counts — no external inputs.
- AC-5: Unit tests cover edge cases: all-new, all-exact, mixed, zero-claims.

## Spec References
- §7.4 DEL-002, §4.2.6 SCH-006
close_reason: Completed: novelty.py with compute_novelty_score(). 21 tests cover all 5 ACs: formula match within 1e-9, zero-claims → 0, range [0,1], inputs-only, edge cases (all-new/exact/contradicting/mixed/negative). 687/687 pass.

title: Implement context command contract (CMD-CTX-001)
description:
## Requirement
context command MUST produce bounded output and include citations to supporting Notes/Sources (CMD-CTX-001). Assembles a Context Pack for a user goal.

## Spec Reference
§5.2.6 CMD-CTX-001

## Inputs
- Optional: goal (string), project (string), tags (array[string]), limit (int), strict (boolean)

## Output data
- items: array[{path, type, title, rationale, citations}]
- traversal_trace: object (bounded traversal record)
- limits_applied: object (e.g., limit, max depth)

## Side Effects
None (read-only).

## Errors
ERR_CONTEXT_EMPTY, ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-CMD-CTX-001-1: Returned items.length is <= limit when limit is provided.
- AC-CMD-CTX-001-2: Every returned item includes at least one citation that resolves to an existing Note path.

## Implementation Notes
- Read-only command with graph traversal
- Must respect traversal bounds and include traversal trace for diagnostics
- Strict mode applies (read-only downgrade semantics per IF-003)

title: Implement sanitization and redaction for egress payloads (SEC-004)
description:
## Context
§9.2 of the spec. When sanitization is enabled, outbound egress payloads must be

## Requirements

  and local absolute paths unless explicitly required by command.
- Fail closed on redact/parse failures (block egress).
- Record redaction_summary in audit event details.
- Include redaction_summary indicating categories applied.

## Acceptance Criteria

  with redaction_summary indicating both categories were applied.
- AC-SEC-004-2: If sanitization fails (induced failure), egress blocked and recorded

title: Enforce draft-first agent outputs invariant (INV-003)
description:
## Requirement

## Spec Reference
§3 INV-003

## Implementation Notes
- Draft scope directories: Inbox/, Reports/, Logs/, Indexes/, Quarantine/

title: Implement Dry Run flag (IF-002)
description:
## Requirement
Commands that can write files MUST support a Dry Run mode (IF-002). When dry_run=true, the command computes and validates planned writes but performs no filesystem mutations.

## Spec Reference
§5.1.1 IF-002

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-IF-002-1: Dry Run returns planned file operations in data.planned_writes and produces no filesystem diffs.

## Implementation Notes
- Planned write schema: {op: enum(write|move|copy|mkdir|delete), path: string (vault-relative), from_path: string|null (required for move|copy|delete), reason: string|null}
- Must be integrated into ingest, graduate, and any future write commands
- Dry run validation must run the same schema checks as live mode

title: Implement Extraction Bundle schema (SCH-008)

title: [SEC] MCP server: shell injection via shell=True in run_command
description: CRITICAL: _run_command in mcp/server.py uses subprocess.run with shell=True, passing untrusted command strings from MCP clients. Any client can execute arbitrary shell commands. Combined with auto_approve being client-controllable, the HITL gate is trivially bypassed. Fix: Remove shell=True, implement command allowlisting, remove auto_approve from MCP tool signatures.

title: Implement stage-scoped pipeline error types with deterministic codes
description:
## Context
Spec §10.1 ERR-001: Pipeline failures MUST be explicit, stage-scoped, and recoverable without manual deletion of Canonical Scope files.

## Work
- Define error enum/struct with `stage` field and deterministic `code` for each pipeline stage.
- Canonical Stage Names (§6.1.2): capture, normalize, fingerprint, extract, compare, delta, propose_queue.
- Ensure each stage returns typed errors that include stage identifier and a stable error code.
- Errors must not leave Canonical Scope in a dirty state — partial artifacts stay in Draft/Quarantine only.
- Implement retry-safe semantics: retrying the same ingestion after a transient failure succeeds without manual cleanup.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-ERR-001-1: For induced failures in each Stage, the returned error includes `stage` set to one of the canonical Stage Names (capture, normalize, fingerprint, extract, compare, delta, propose_queue) and a deterministic `code`.
- AC-ERR-001-2: Retrying the same ingestion after a transient failure produces a successful run without requiring manual cleanup of Canonical Scope.

## Cross-section dependencies
- depends_on_section: §6 (pipeline stages definition, §6.1.2 canonical Stage Names)
- depends_on_section: §4 (vault data model, canonical vs draft scope)
close_reason: Implemented errors.py: PipelineError, StageName enum (7 stages), ERROR_CODES registry (18 codes), convenience constructors. 45 tests covering AC-ERR-001-1 + AC-ERR-001-2. Full suite 462/462 pass.

title: Implement triage scoring with deterministic formula, buckets, and hysteresis
description:
## Context
Spec §12.4 MVP3 Resolution TODO-Q-MVP3-1 (council Option C): Governed deterministic triage lifecycle with hysteresis.

## Work
- Implement triage_score formula: clamp01(0.45*conflict_factor + 0.25*support_gap + 0.20*novelty + 0.10*staleness).
- Implement bucket classification: dense (>=0.67), mixed (0.34-0.67), watery (<0.34).
- Implement hysteresis rules:
  - dense -> mixed only after 2 consecutive evaluations with score < 0.62.
  - watery -> mixed on first evaluation with score >= 0.42.
- Deterministic outputs: same input yields same bucket across runs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-MVP3-1-TRIAGE-1: Triage score computation matches the formula clamp01(0.45*conflict_factor + 0.25*support_gap + 0.20*novelty + 0.10*staleness) exactly for a known fixture.
- AC-MVP3-1-TRIAGE-2: Bucket thresholds classify correctly: dense (>=0.67), mixed (0.34-0.67), watery (<0.34).
- AC-MVP3-1-TRIAGE-3: Hysteresis fixture demonstrates dense->mixed requires 2 consecutive sub-0.62 evaluations; single sub-0.62 does NOT transition.
- AC-MVP3-1-TRIAGE-4: Hysteresis fixture demonstrates watery->mixed transitions on first evaluation with score >= 0.42.
- AC-MVP3-1-TRIAGE-5: Same input yields identical bucket and score across repeated runs (determinism).

## Cross-section dependencies
- depends_on_section: §7 (conflict_factor, support_gap, novelty, staleness derivations)
close_reason: Completed: triage scoring with formula, buckets, and hysteresis. 39 tests covering all 5 ACs

title: Enforce human authority over canon invariant (INV-002)
description:

§3 INV-002

title: Implement Git Mode with per-packet commit granularity (REV-004)
description:
## Context
§8.3.1 of the spec. When Git Mode is enabled, promotions applied from Review Packets
must create exactly one git commit per Source packet apply batch.

## Requirements
- REV-004: Git Mode config in Config/review_policy.yaml at git_mode.enabled (default false).
- graduate --from_digest produces exactly one commit per Source packet apply batch.
- Commit subject schema: graduate packet=<packet_id> source=<source_id> run_ids=<sorted_csv>
- Commit body includes applied queue_id values in deterministic lexical order.
- If Git Mode enabled and commit/write fails, promotion apply must fail atomically
  with no partial canonical mutations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-REV-004-1: Fixture applying promotions for two different Source packets in a single
  run produces exactly two new git commits.
- AC-REV-004-2: Fixture applying promotions for one Source packet produces exactly one
  new commit whose diff contains only changes attributable to that packet's promoted items.
- AC-REV-004-3: Each commit subject matches the schema: graduate packet=<packet_id> source=<source_id> run_ids=<sorted_csv_run_ids>.
- AC-REV-004-4: Each commit body includes applied queue_id values in deterministic lexical order.
- AC-REV-004-5: If commit or write fails during Git Mode promotion, no partial canonical mutations persist (atomicity).

## Cross-section Dependencies
- depends_on_section: §8.3 (REV-003 promotion semantics, prerequisite)
close_reason: Completed: git_mode.py with PacketPromotion, commit_packet_promotion(), apply_git_mode_promotions(). 13 tests covering all 5 ACs. 1818/1819 pass.

title: Implement shared frontmatter schema validation (SCH-001)
description:
## Requirement
Every Note MUST include the shared frontmatter keys: type, id, status, created, updated (SCH-001).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-001-1: A schema validator rejects any Note missing a required shared key and reports which key is missing.
- AC-SCH-001-2: `created` and `updated` parse as UTC ISO-8601 and validator rejects invalid formats.
- AC-SCH-001-3: If `confidence` exists, validator rejects values outside [0.0..1.0].
- AC-SCH-001-4: If `last_reviewed_at` exists, validator rejects invalid datetime formats.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.1

## Implementation Notes
- Required keys: type (enum), id (string), status (enum), created (datetime), updated (datetime)
- Optional keys: tags (list[string]), confidence (number 0-1), last_reviewed_at (datetime)
- Validators MUST ignore unknown keys (forward compatibility)
- type enum: source | claim | concept | question | project | moc
- status enum: draft | reviewed | canon
- This is the foundation schema validator — all note-type-specific validators build on it
close_reason: Implemented schema.py with validate_shared_frontmatter/validate_shared_frontmatter_strict. 42 tests in test_schema.py covering all 4 ACs. Full suite 231/231 green.

title: Validate MVP1 end-to-end: ingest, dedupe, delta, review digest, auto-lane

title: Build p95 benchmark suite for MVP1/MVP2 latency targets

title: [BUG] Comparator: NaN from custom similarity fn silently misclassifies
description: HIGH: compare_claim does not validate similarity_fn return values. If a custom function returns NaN, the comparison result is always 0.0 (NaN > best_sim is False), silently classifying all claims as NEW regardless of actual similarity. Fix: Validate sim_fn return is in [0.0, 1.0] range.
close_reason: Fixed: added NaN/infinity validation on similarity_fn return in compare_claim — raises ValueError for non-finite results. Tests pass.

title: Implement egress_policy.yaml config with mode, burn-in, and transition tracking
description:
## Context
§9.2 resolved TODO-Q-SEC-1. Config file for egress policy state.

## Requirements
- Config file: Config/egress_policy.yaml
- Keys: mode (report_only|enforce), burn_in_started_at (ISO-8601 UTC),
  last_transition_at (ISO-8601 UTC|null), transitioned_by (string|null),
  transition_reason (string|null).
- Burn-in elapsed days computed from burn_in_started_at at evaluation time;
  no scheduler required.
- Transition report_only -> enforce is explicit only (command or config change),
  must emit audit event per SEC-003.
- Passing 14 days does NOT auto-flip mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: Missing Config/egress_policy.yaml defaults to report_only mode.
- AC-2: Config with mode=enforce is loaded and respected by egress evaluation.
- AC-3: burn_in_started_at is used to compute elapsed days deterministically
  at evaluation time (no timer/scheduler).
- AC-4: Invalid mode value (e.g., mode=partial) produces ERR_SCHEMA_VALIDATION in Strict Mode.
- AC-5: Config round-trips: writing then reading produces identical values.
- AC-6: last_transition_at and transitioned_by are null until first explicit transition.

title: Implement deterministic test mode infrastructure (TST-G-002)
description:
Implement Deterministic Test Mode for golden testing.

**Spec ref:** §13.4 TST-G-002

**Requirements:**
- Support either fixed clock + stable ID generation, OR a documented normalization step that removes nondeterminism before comparison
- Deterministic mode affects only nondeterministic fields (timestamps/IDs) and does NOT change semantic classifications (match classes)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-TST-G-002-1: Running the same fixture twice yields byte-identical normalized outputs for golden comparison
- AC-TST-G-002-2: Deterministic mode affects only nondeterministic fields (timestamps/IDs) and does not change semantic classifications (match classes)

**depends_on_section:** §4 (Vault Data Model for schema), §6 (Ingestion Pipeline for pipeline stages)

This is a foundational bead — golden fixtures, unit tests, integration tests, and regression tests all depend on deterministic mode being available.

title: Implement Delta Report schema and persistence (SCH-006)
description:
## Requirement
Each Ingestion Job MUST persist exactly one Delta Report per Run ID in YAML under Reports/Delta/ (SCH-006).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-006-1: After any ingestion attempt that begins extraction and produces a schema-valid Extraction Bundle, a Delta Report exists for the Run ID even if later stages fail.
- AC-SCH-006-2: Delta Report YAML always includes required top-level keys and includes empty arrays explicitly (keys present even if empty).
- AC-SCH-006-3: Validator rejects Delta Reports with `novelty_score` outside [0..1].
- AC-SCH-006-4: Each Match Record includes required keys and `match_class` equals its containing group key.
- AC-SCH-006-5: `pipeline_status` is `completed` on success, `failed_after_extraction` when failure occurs after extraction begins, `failed_before_extraction` otherwise.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.6

## Implementation Notes
- Required top-level keys: run_id, source_id, created_at, source_revision, pipeline_status, counts, novelty_score, match_groups, conflicts, warnings, failures, new_links, follow_up_questions
- source_revision: normalized_locator, fingerprint, prior_fingerprint (nullable)
- counts: total_extracted_claims, exact_count, near_duplicate_count, supporting_count, contradicting_count, new_count
- match_groups: EXACT, NEAR_DUPLICATE, SUPPORTING, CONTRADICTING, NEW (each an array)
- Match Record: extracted_claim_key, match_class, similarity (0-1), existing_claim_id (nullable for NEW)
- Conflict Record: new_extracted_claim_key, existing_claim_id, evidence object
- This is a complex schema — consider a dedicated DeltaReport builder class
- depends_on_section: §6 (pipeline stages determine when to write)
- depends_on_section: §7 (dedupe engine populates match_groups)

title: [BUG] Sanitize email regex misses Unicode/IDN addresses (data leak)
description: HIGH: Email regex in sanitize.py only matches ASCII characters. Emails with Unicode local parts or internationalized domain names pass through unredacted. Since this is the egress sanitizer, failing to redact valid emails is a PII data leak. Fix: Expand character class to include Unicode letters, or use a more comprehensive email detection pattern.
close_reason: Fixed: expanded email regex to Unicode-aware pattern using \w with re.UNICODE flag. Tests pass.

title: Implement Extraction Bundle schema (SCH-008)
description:
## Requirement
Each Ingestion Job that completes the Extract stage MUST persist at least one Extraction Bundle artifact in YAML under Inbox/Sources/ (SCH-008).

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-SCH-008-1: For a successful extraction, an Extraction Bundle YAML file exists under Inbox/Sources/ and validates against this schema.
- AC-SCH-008-2: Validator rejects Extraction Bundles that omit any required top-level key or that contain any claim with empty `claim_text`.
- AC-SCH-008-3: If `claims` is empty, `warnings` contains an entry with code `WARN_NO_CLAIMS_EXTRACTED`.

## Spec Reference
§4.2.8

## Implementation Notes
- Required top-level keys: run_id, source_id, created_at, gist, bullets, claims, entities, definitions, warnings
- claims entry required keys: extracted_claim_key, claim_text, claim_type, polarity, provenance
- Optional claim keys: suggested_note_id (must conform to NAM-001 if present), notes
- warnings: code + message + optional details
- depends_on_section: §6 (Extract stage produces this artifact)

title: Enforce canonical notes as human-readable YAML+Markdown with no binary blobs
description:
## Context
Spec §11.1 MIG-001: All Canonical Notes MUST remain human-readable and git-diff friendly.

## Work
- Add validation that canonical notes contain only YAML frontmatter + Markdown body, no binary blobs.
- Ensure schema changes that add new fields do NOT require rewriting unchanged canonical notes (additive-only field policy).
- Add a lint/check that can be run to verify all canonical notes comply with this requirement.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC-MIG-001-2: Schema changes that add fields do not require rewriting unchanged canonical notes.
